---
title: "HistoryHandlerElement"
lightbox: true
description: "Maintains raw history, emits projected context, and coordinates summarization"
---

`HistoryHandlerElement` manages the conversation ledger used by downstream chat
flows. It stores raw `MessagePayload`, `ToolsResponsePayload`, and accepted
summary artifacts, then emits projected context for elements such as
`ContextBuilderElement` and `LLMChatElement`.

The element separates three concerns:

- Raw history retention, capped by `history_token_limit`.
- Context projection, capped by `context_token_limit`.
- Summarization coordination, driven by `summary_token_threshold`.

It does not call an LLM itself. When older unsummarized entries cross the summary
threshold, it emits a structured summary request (`StructuredPayload` with
`type: summary_request`) for a `SummarizerElement` and later accepts a structured
summary artifact (`type: summary`).

## Instantiation

**Arguments:**

`show_tool_responses: bool = False`<br>
<span class="tab">Whether to include tool response payload views in the context view.</span>
`persist: bool = False`<br>
<span class="tab">Whether to persist messages and tool responses to a SQLite database.</span>
`db_path: str = None`<br>
<span class="tab">Custom path for the history database file when `persist=True`.</span>
`history_token_limit: int = 32000`<br>
<span class="tab">The max number of raw tokens to keep in the history ledger.</span>
`context_token_limit: int = 16000`<br>
<span class="tab">The max number of projected tokens to emit on `context_output`.</span>
`summary_token_threshold: int = 8000`<br>
<span class="tab">Raw-token distance from the newest entry at which unsummarized entries become eligible for summarization. Set to `0` to disable summary candidates.</span>
`projection_tiers: dict = None`<br>
<span class="tab">Optional tier configuration for projecting older entries before emitting context.</span>
`tokenizer_model: str = 'gpt-4o'`<br>
<span class="tab">The tokenizer model to use for token length estimation. (Using tiktoken)</span>

### Input Ports

| Port Name                 | Payload Type                                                 | Behavior                                                                      |
|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `payload_emit_input` | `MessagePayload`, `ToolsResponsePayload`, or lists of those payloads | Loads supported payloads into history, then emits projected context and any summary request. |
| `payload_input` | `MessagePayload`, `ToolsResponsePayload`, or lists of those payloads | Loads supported payloads into history without emitting context or summary requests. |
| `summary_input` | `StructuredPayload` (`type: summary`) or other payload with a model | Accepts a summary artifact, appends it to history, and marks `data["source_entry_ids"]` when present on structured summaries. |

: {.hover}

### Output Ports

| Port Name               | Payload Type            | Behavior                                                      |
|-------------------------|-------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| `context_output` | `list` | Emits the current projected context payloads in chronological order. |
| `summary_candidate_output` | `StructuredPayload` (`type: summary_request`) | Emits raw unsummarized history spans that are eligible for summarization. |

: {.hover}

## Summarization Flow

The history handler only selects candidates and records returned summaries. The
LLM call belongs in a connected `SummarizerElement`:

```python
history_handler_el.ports.summary_candidate_output > summarizer_el.ports.summary_request_emit_input
summarizer_el.ports.summary_output > history_handler_el.ports.summary_input
```

The structured summary request contains `source_payloads`, `source_entry_ids`,
and optional `instructions`. See [Structured Summary Contract](../../explanation/StructuredSummary.qmd).

When a structured summary artifact (`type: summary`) returns on `summary_input`,
the model appends it to the ledger and marks exactly `data["source_entry_ids"]` as
summarized. Those entries will not be selected again as summary candidates.

:::: {.callout-note}
Summarized entries are still part of the raw ledger until normal token-limit
eviction. The summary marker prevents repeated summarization; context projection
still decides what gets emitted on `context_output`.
::::

## Typical Chat Wiring

Use `payload_emit_input` for turns that should refresh context immediately:

```python
chat_interface_el.ports.user_message_output > history_handler_el.ports.payload_emit_input
history_handler_el.ports.context_output > context_builder_el.ports.history
context_builder_el.ports.messages_output > llm_chat_el.ports.messages_emit_input
llm_chat_el.ports.message_output > history_handler_el.ports.payload_emit_input
```

Use `payload_input` when another event will trigger context emission later.

### Views

{{< var views.styling >}}

| View Name     | Description                                                                                                                    | Image                                     |
|---------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| context_view  | Displays a column of message and optional tool response history entries.<br>**Args:**<br>`title: str = 'Current History'`<br><span class="tab">The header text shown above the history container.</span><br>`column_css: list = []`<br><span class="tab">CSS stylesheets for the main column layout.</span><br>`container_css: list = []`<br><span class="tab">CSS stylesheets for the scrollable context container.</span><br>`title_css: list = []`<br><span class="tab">CSS stylesheets for the title Markdown pane.</span><br>`title_visible: bool = True`<br><span class="tab">Whether the title header is visible.</span> | ![ContextView](context_view.jpg){.lightbox} |
